explicit_text: I was born and lived in Minneapolis, Minnesota until my junior year of high school. My father was an attorney there with an office overlooking Nicollet Park. Minneapolis was where I developed my love for baseball, watching players like Ted Williams from my father's office window.

explicit_text: In the summer of 1942, my family moved to Milwaukee, Wisconsin, where I attended Shorewood High School. Milwaukee was also where I registered for the draft and was inducted into the Army at the Buffalo Street Induction Center. It was a pivotal place for me as I transitioned from high school to military service.

explicit_text: After Camp Grant, I was sent to Fort Hamilton in Brooklyn, New York, where I joined the 113th Infantry Regiment.
